# terrible_community_postgresql_postgresql_set (Data Source)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this data source against
- `name` (String) Name of PostgreSQL server parameter. Pay attention that parameters are case sensitive (see examples below).

### Optional

- `login_db` (String) Name of database to connect. The `db` alias is deprecated and will be removed in version 5.0.0.
- `reset` (Boolean) Restore parameter to initial state (boot_val). Mutually exclusive with *value*.
- `session_role` (String) Switch to session_role after connecting. The specified session_role must be a role that the current login_user is a member of. Permissions checking for SQL commands is carried out as though the session_role were the one that had logged in originally.
- `trust_input` (Boolean) If `false`, check whether values of parameters are potentially dangerous. It makes sense to use `false` only when SQL injections are possible.
- `value` (String) Parameter value to set. To remove parameter string from postgresql.auto.conf and reload the server configuration you must pass *value=default*. With *value=default* the playbook always returns changed is true.
